title: RISK: connection_manager never logs reconnection attempts after first; no retry diagnostics
description: core/src/transport.rs lines 226-229: on Err(..) from open_authenticated_ws(), only a warn\!() is logged with no context (url, error details, attempt count). If reconnection loops silently for hours (e.g., relay permanently down but client doesn't exit), a solo maintainer sees no logs indicating the failure rate or duration. The delay_secs backoff is capped at 60s (line 252), but there's no final-failure log ('gave up after N attempts') or exponential-backoff-cap warning. If the relay is unavailable for a day, the operator has no way to know the client was reconnecting, vs. crashed, vs. never tried. Risk: silent reconnection loops with no operator visibility or diagnostic trail.
close_reason: Out of scope for JSON-RPC migration epic. Real concern but not introduced by this epic — file as separate issue if prioritized.
